message:
feat(stream): cooperative budgets for Buffered, BufferUnordered, and Merge

Add per-poll cooperative yielding to three stream combinators that
were previously unbounded in their per-poll work:

- buffered.rs: Two new budgets for the Buffered and BufferUnordered
  combinators:
  * BUFFERED_ADMISSION_BUDGET (1024): caps how many new futures are
    pulled from the upstream stream per poll, preventing an always-
    ready source from filling the entire in-flight queue in one turn.
  * BUFFERED_POLL_BUDGET (1024): caps how many in-flight futures are
    polled per turn, preventing large buffer limits from monopolizing
    the executor when many futures are pending.
  Also adds next_poll_index for round-robin fairness across in-flight
  futures, ensuring no future is starved when the buffer exceeds the
  poll budget. Includes comprehensive tests with PendingOnceFuture
  and AlwaysReadyPendingFutureStream verifying both budgets and the
  self-wake mechanism for both ordered and unordered variants.

- merge.rs: MERGE_COOPERATIVE_POLL_BUDGET (64) caps how many child
  streams are scanned per poll. Large merge sets (e.g. fan-in from
  hundreds of connections) previously walked every child stream on
  every poll. Now yields after 64 scans with a resume cursor that
  rotates across polls for fairness. Includes test verifying cursor
  advancement and self-wake behavior.

- collect.rs: Minor test improvement — uses matches!() pattern
  instead of == with .clone() for cleaner assertion.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
